source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Question: 54
The histogram represents the heights of males in the United States between the ages 20â29. The mean is 69.6 inches, and the standard deviation is 2.6 inches (approximately normal). Three adult males are selected from the group. Their heights are 75 inches, 63 inches, and 79 inches. Determine which heights, if any, are unusual.
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: All heights are unusual since they are more than two standard deviations from the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: 2.08, â2.54, and 3.62.

choice_map.A: The 79-inch height is the only one that is unusual because it is more than three standard deviations above the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: 2.08, â2.54, and 3.62.
choice_map.B: The 79-inch and 63-inch heights are the only ones that are unusual because they are more than two standard deviations from the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: 2.00, â2.54, and 3.62.
choice_map.C: All heights are unusual since they are more than two standard deviations from the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: â2.08, â2.54, and 3.62.
choice_map.D: All heights are unusual since they are more than two standard deviations from the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: 2.08, â2.54, and 3.62.
choice_map.E: All heights are unusual since they are more than two standard deviations from the mean. The z-scores, respectively, are: 2.08, 2.54, and 3.62.

extraction_notes: answer field is numeric; interpreted as a zero-based index into the choices array
image field contains a PIL.Image object representation, not a path or URL

multi_step: Solving requires computing z-scores for three separate heights using the given mean and standard deviation.
The result then requires comparing each computed value against an unusualness rule (more than two standard deviations from the mean).
This is more than a single lookup, but still a short, structured quantitative reasoning task.
verifiability: The answer is numerically checkable: each height can be converted to a z-score and classified by a clear threshold.
The provided answer includes explicit z-scores and a concrete set of unusual heights, enabling deterministic verification.
The item also has multiple-choice options, which further constrains and clarifies the expected output.
